name: MeshFace
summary: Represents the values of the four indices of a mesh face quad. If the third and fourth values are the same, this face represents a triangle.

constructors.name: MeshFace(Int32, Int32, Int32)
constructors.signature: public MeshFace(int a, int b, int c)
constructors.summary: Constructs a new triangular Mesh face.

constructors.name: MeshFace(Int32, Int32, Int32, Int32)
constructors.signature: public MeshFace(int a, int b, int c, int d)
constructors.summary: Constructs a new quadrangular Mesh face.

methods.name: CompareTo
methods.summary: Compares this MeshFace with another MeshFace and returns a value of 1, 0, or -1, referring to dictionary order. Index evaluation priority is first A, then B, then C, then D.

params.name: other
params.doc: The other MeshFace to use in comparison.

returns.doc: 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this < other. Priority is for index of corner A first, then B, then C, then D.+1: otherwise.

methods.name: Equals(Object)
methods.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the other object obj has precisely the same value as the current one.

params.name: obj
params.doc: Any object the represents the other mesh face for comparison.

returns.doc: true if obj is a mesh face that, also orderly, equals to the present one; otherwise false.

methods.name: Equals(MeshFace)
methods.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the other mesh face has precisely the same value as the current one.

params.name: other
params.doc: The other mesh face for comparison.

returns.doc: true if the other face is, also orderly, equal to the present one; otherwise false.

methods.name: Flip
methods.summary: Reverses the orientation of the face by swapping corners. The first corner is always maintained.

methods.name: GetHashCode
methods.summary: Returns a runtime-stable hash code for the current mesh face. You are not allowed to rely on persistence of this hash code in serialization, but for each version of RhinoCommon, this hash code will be the same for each mesh face.

returns.doc: A non-unique integer that represents this mesh face.

methods.name: IsValid()
methods.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is considered to be valid. Note that even valid mesh faces could potentially be invalid in the context of a specific Mesh, if one or more of the corner indices exceeds the number of vertices on the mesh. If you want to perform a complete validity check, use IsValid(int) instead.

methods.name: IsValid(Int32)
methods.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is considered to be valid. Unlike the simple IsValid function, this function takes upper bound indices into account.

params.name: vertexCount
params.doc: Number of vertices in the mesh that this face is a part of.

returns.doc: true if the face is considered valid, false if not.

methods.name: IsValid(Point3d[])
methods.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is considered to be valid. Unlike the simple IsValid function, this function takes actual point locations into account.

params.name: points
params.doc: Array of vertices with which to validate the face.

returns.doc: true if the face is considered valid, false if not.

methods.name: IsValidEx
methods.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is considered to be valid. Unlike the simple IsValid function, this function takes actual point locations into account.

params.name: points
params.doc: Array of vertices with which to validate the face.

returns.doc: true if the face is considered valid, false if not.

methods.name: Repair
methods.summary: Attempts to repair this mesh face by taking both face indexes and actual vertex locations into account.
methods.remarks: This function assumes the face is invalid and attempts to repair unconditionally. It is only worthwhile to call Repair on faces where IsValid returns false.

params.name: points
params.doc: Array of vertices with which to consider when repairing the face.

returns.doc: true if the face was repaired, false if not.

methods.name: RepairEx
methods.summary: Attempts to repair this mesh face by taking both face indexes and actual vertex locations into account.
methods.remarks: This function assumes the face is invalid and attempts to repair unconditionally. It is only worthwhile to call Repair on faces where IsValid returns false. This function is the analog of UnsafeNativeMethods.ON_MeshFace_Repair done completely here to avoid copying the point array.

params.name: points
params.doc: Array of vertices with which to consider when repairing the face.

returns.doc: true if the face was repaired, false if not.

methods.name: Set(Int32, Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public void Set(int a, int b, int c)
methods.summary: Sets all the corners for this face as a triangle.

methods.name: Set(Int32, Int32, Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public void Set(int a, int b, int c, int d)
methods.summary: Sets all the corners for this face as a quad.

methods.name: ToString
methods.summary: Returns a string representation for this MeshFace. This is to provide a meaningful visualization of this structure and is subject to change in newer releases.

methods.name: Equality
methods.signature: public static bool operator ==(MeshFace a, MeshFace b)
methods.summary: Determines whether two MeshFace structures have equal values.

returns.doc: true if the indices of the two points are exactly equal; otherwise false.

methods.name: Inequality
methods.signature: public static bool operator !=(MeshFace a, MeshFace b)
methods.summary: Determines whether two MeshFace structures have different values.

returns.doc: true if the indices of the two points are in any way different; otherwise false.

properties.name: A
properties.summary: Gets or sets the first corner index of the mesh face.

properties.name: B
properties.summary: Gets or sets the second corner index of the mesh face.

properties.name: C
properties.summary: Gets or sets the third corner index of the mesh face.

properties.name: D
properties.summary: Gets or sets the fourth corner index of the mesh face. If D equals C, the mesh face is considered to be a triangle rather than a quad.

properties.name: IsQuad
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is a quad. A mesh face is considered to be a triangle when C does not equal D, thus it is possible for an Invalid mesh face to also be a quad.

properties.name: IsTriangle
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this mesh face is a triangle. A mesh face is considered to be a triangle when C equals D, thus it is possible for an Invalid mesh face to also be a triangle.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Gets or sets the vertex index associated with an entry in this face.

properties.name: Unset
properties.summary: Gets an Unset MeshFace. Unset faces have Int32.MinValue for all corner indices.
